Operation 'updateAuthRule' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too minimal and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved description specifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) what the request contains and that partial updates are supported, (3) what is returned (AuthRule object), and (4) side effects (immediate application to associated cards). This enables AI agents to understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

data.current_description: Updates an existing authorization rule.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing authorization rule identified by auth_rule_token. Accepts an UpdateAuthRuleRequest containing the fields to modify (partial updates supported). Returns the updated AuthRule object with all current properties. Changes take effect immediately and apply to all cards associated with this rule.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved description specifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) what the request contains and that partial updates are supported, (3) what is returned (AuthRule object), and (4) side effects (immediate application to associated cards). This enables AI agents to understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

message: Operation 'deleteAuthRule' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks essential context for safe operation execution. The improved description clarifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) the permanence and irreversibility of deletion, (3) the side effect on associated cards (restrictions no longer applied), and (4) the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of the operation and its downstream effects before execution.

data.current_description: Deletes an authorization rule.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an authorization rule identified by auth_rule_token. This operation removes the rule permanently and cannot be undone. Cards previously governed by this rule will no longer have its restrictions applied. Returns a 200 status on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks essential context for safe operation execution. The improved description clarifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) the permanence and irreversibility of deletion, (3) the side effect on associated cards (restrictions no longer applied), and (4) the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of the operation and its downstream effects before execution.

data.current_description: Returns a paginated list of transactions. All amounts are in the smallest unit of their respective currency.

data.current_description: Creates a new virtual or physical card associated with an account.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new virtual or physical card associated with an account. Accepts a CreateCardRequest specifying card type, account association, and optional metadata. Operation 'updateAuthRule' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too minimal and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved description specifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) what the request contains and that partial updates are supported, (3) what is returned (AuthRule object), and (4) side effects (immediate application to associated cards). This enables AI agents to understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

data.current_description: Updates an existing authorization rule.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing authorization rule identified by auth_rule_token. Accepts an UpdateAuthRuleRequest containing the fields to modify (partial updates supported). Returns the updated AuthRule object with all current properties. Changes take effect immediately and apply to all cards associated with this rule.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved description specifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) what the request contains and that partial updates are supported, (3) what is returned (AuthRule object), and (4) side effects (immediate application to associated cards). This enables AI agents to understand the operation's full scope and consequences.

message: Operation 'deleteAuthRule' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks essential context for safe operation execution. The improved description clarifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) the permanence and irreversibility of deletion, (3) the side effect on associated cards (restrictions no longer applied), and (4) the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of the operation and its downstream effects before execution.

data.current_description: Deletes an authorization rule.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an authorization rule identified by auth_rule_token. This operation removes the rule permanently and cannot be undone. Cards previously governed by this rule will no longer have its restrictions applied. Returns a 200 status on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks essential context for safe operation execution. The improved description clarifies: (1) what identifies the rule (auth_rule_token), (2) the permanence and irreversibility of deletion, (3) the side effect on associated cards (restrictions no longer applied), and (4) the response behavior. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of the operation and its downstream effects before execution.

data.current_description: Creates a new virtual or physical card associated with an account.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new virtual or physical card associated with an account. Accepts a CreateCardRequest specifying card type, account association, and optional metadata. Returns the newly created Card object with assigned card_token. The card is created in an active state by default and can be used immediately for transactions.
data.reasoning: Missing critical details about request parameters (what fields are required/optional in CreateCardRequest), the response structure (card_token assignment), and initial card state. AI agents need to understand what inputs are required and what the created card's initial state will be.

data.current_description: Updates a card's properties such as state or memo.
data.description_suggestion: Updates a card's properties using the provided UpdateCardRequest. Supports modifying card state (e.g., active, paused, closed) and memo fields. Requires the card_token path parameter. Returns the updated Card object reflecting all changes. Partial updates are supported - only provided fields will be modified.
data.reasoning: Current description uses vague language ('such as') and doesn't clarify which properties are updatable, whether updates are partial or full, or what the response contains. AI agents need explicit details about updateable fields and update semantics.

data.current_summary: Provision a card for digital wallet
data.current_description: Provisions a card for use with a digital wallet (Apple Pay, Google Pay, etc.).
data.description_suggestion: Provisions a card for use with a digital wallet (Apple Pay, Google Pay, etc.). Accepts a ProvisionCardRequest specifying wallet provider and device details. Requires the card_token path parameter. Returns a ProvisionCardResponse containing provisioning status and wallet-specific identifiers. The card must be in an active state to be provisioned.
data.reasoning: Missing details about what ProvisionCardRequest contains, what the response includes, and prerequisites (card state requirements). AI agents need to understand the request structure, response format, and any preconditions for successful provisioning.
